answer:
The function is defined when the denominator is nonzero and the arguments of the logarithms are positive.  
We need \(x-2 > 0\) so \(x > 2\), and \(2 - \log(x-2) > 0\) so \(\log(x-2) < 2\) which gives \(x-2 < 10^2 = 100\), i.e., \(x < 102\).  
Also, the denominator \(\log(2 - \log(x-2)) \neq 0\) means \(2 - \log(x-2) \neq 1\), so \(\log(x-2) \neq 1\) and \(x-2 \neq 10\), i.e., \(x \neq 12\).  
Thus the domain is \((2, 12) \cup (12, 102)\).

\boxed{(2,12)\cup(12,102)}
